title: Neobakeria angustifolia
taxonomy: Neobakeria angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. The plant is native to South America, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes coffee and gardenias. The classification of Neobakeria angustifolia provides important information about its relationships with other plants and its evolutionary history.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Neobakeria angustifolia. However, I can provide a general paragraph on a similar-sounding plant, Neobakeria (which is actually a synonym of the plant species, Calea angustifolia also known as Nierembergia angustifolia). 

Calea angustifolia, also known as Nierembergia angustifolia, is a flowering plant in the phytolaccaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving anxiety and stress, and supporting the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Calea angustifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and well-being.
